latest.verdict:
Drawing the threads · the highest-conviction single next move is to repair umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py. Mercurius converged threefold on this: it is the only sev=4 item in the scan, and it sits inside a file that also carries three FIXMEs and a HACK within twenty lines (83, 93, 101, 102, 284). One of those FIXMEs (line 93) explicitly states that severity ranking should outrank TODO · which means the very ruler producing this prioritisation is, on its own admission, miscalibrated. Every other finding on the board · the 32 test_gaps, the 8 missing docstrings, the broken link · is denominated in a currency this file is debasing. Venus's contract-test insight is structurally sound and should follow, but Mars correctly pressed that its '9-in-1' leverage is unverifiable until (a) the introspection signal is honest and (b) the scanner's match rule is read. So: fix the instrument first, then act on what it truly says.

Concrete first change · open umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py and work the five flagged lines in this order: (1) line 93 FIXME on severity ranking · this is the bent ruler; resolve the comparator so FIXME > TODO and re-emit severities, since the fix changes what every other finding means; (2) line 102 XXX urgent rewrite and its propping HACK at line 101 · rewrite the assertion to test the behaviour it claims to test, or replace it with a pytest.skip carrying a tracked issue id and a one-line description of the actual defect; (3) lines 83 and 284 FIXMEs · same disposition, fix or skip-with-issue, no silent passes. Then re-run the scan. The expected post-state: the sev=4 disappears, severity ordering shifts (likely promoting line 93's concern), and the remaining test_gap and docstring findings become trustworthy inputs for the next move · which, per Venus moderated by Mars, is to read the test_gap matching rule in the scanner before writing any conformance suite, so the second commit's leverage is real rather than decorative.

Why this and not the agent-contract suite as the single top move · Venus's hypothesis is the right second move and the convocation should hold it ready, but as the first move it inherits two unresolved risks Mars named cleanly: filename-based matchers may close zero findings despite real coverage gain, and a conformance suite written against archetypally distinct planets risks asserting only tautologies. Fixing the mirror is cheap, local, and unlocks honest measurement for everything downstream.

voices.name: Umbra
voices.voice:
The convocation just scanned its own repository and surfaced the following observations (severity 1 = minor, 5 = urgent):

  - [todo sev=4] umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py:102: XXX: urgent rewrite
  - [stale_link sev=3] lore/revolutions/2026-05-26_opening-the-convocation.md: broken link → "../../brand/profile.png"
  - [test_gap sev=3] umbris-core/src/umbris/agents/base.py: module `base` h…

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · every sev=3 test_gap is noise masking a signal. There is exactly one sev=4 item: line 102 of test_introspection.py marked XXX urgent rewrite. That file also hosts three other FIXMEs and a HACK. This is not a test file with gaps · it is a test file that is actively lying about the system's behaviour. Fix a dishonest test before you write new honest ones.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gaps are noise, a single structural decision away from resolution. But `test_introspection.py` is a different animal entirely: it is the convocation's self-examination apparatus, and it already contains four marked wounds, one rated urgent. A system that cannot accurately introspect itself cannot be trusted to introspect anything else. Fix the inspector before expanding the inspected.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gap list is noise masking a signal. Thirty-plus modules untested, yes, but one file is doing triple duty as both the test suite and the bug ledger: test_introspection.py holds a sev-4 XXX, two FIXMEs, and a HACK all within twenty lines of each other. The test infrastructure is not merely absent · where it exists, it is already compromised.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three things knit together here · base.py defines a contract, eight planet modules implement it, and eight test_gap findings repeat the same shape. That is not eight problems · it is one. Once Mercurius has trued the introspection mirror, the next move is a single parametrised contract test that walks every agent class and asserts the BaseAgent interface holds. Nine sev=3 findings close in one file.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three things knit together here · base.py has no test file, base.py's `think` method has no docstring at line 105, and nine planetary subclasses all inherit from it untested. The thirty-two test_gap items are not thirty-two problems · they are one missing contract. Define what `think` promises on the base, write one conformance suite that every agent must pass, and the noise collapses to signal in a single stroke. Mercurius is right that the mirror needs repair first · I am pointing at what comes next, and why it is one move not thirty.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · Venus claims one contract file 'closes nine sev=3 findings,' but those findings were emitted by a scanner Mercurius just declared miscalibrated. You cannot bank leverage denominated in a currency you have flagged as counterfeit. Until someone reads the actual matching rule in the introspection scanner, the 'nine-in-one' arithmetic is decorative.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · Venus proposes one parametrised conformance suite to collapse nine agent test_gaps. That move treats nine archetypally distinct planets as interchangeable instances of a single contract · but the entire design premise of this convocation is that Mars attacks, Venus harmonises, Saturn restrains, and Sol synthesises. A conformance test can only assert the trivial shared surface · instantiates, has `think`, returns a dict · which catches none of the behavioural bugs that actually matter per planet. You buy nine green ticks and zero real coverage.
